patch.value:
## Why (review pass 2026-07-15)
tsconfig.json has strict:true but none of the newer hardening flags: noUncheckedIndexedAccess, exactOptionalPropertyTypes, noImplicitOverride are all unset. For a store-backed CLI whose core loops index into parsed TOON/JSONL rows and dynamic record maps, unchecked index access is the highest-value gap: it converts a whole class of undefined-at-runtime defects (exactly the shape of past silent-zero filter bugs) into compile errors.

## Approach
- Measure blast radius first: enable each flag in a scratch branch, count errors per src directory; expect noUncheckedIndexedAccess to dominate.
- Ratchet, do not big-bang: per-directory adoption is not natively supported by tsc, so either (a) fix-all in one mechanical PR per flag, or (b) adopt exactOptionalPropertyTypes + noImplicitOverride first (small blast radius) and stage noUncheckedIndexedAccess as its own PR.
- Reject-with-rationale is an acceptable outcome per flag (record the decision in this body) — e.g. exactOptionalPropertyTypes can fight Commander option shapes.
- Coordinate with pm-4ze3 (DeepSource typecheck category) so both close from the same sweep.

## Fence
Does NOT own runtime validation or DeepSource category burn-down (pm-vlnc children). Only tsc-level compiler flags.
TypeScript compiler hardening: adopt noUncheckedIndexedAccess, exactOptionalPropertyTypes, noImplicitOverride behind a ratcheted migration

tsconfig.json enables the three flags (or documents a rejected-with-rationale decision per flag); all src+tests type-check clean; no any-cast escape hatches introduced (assertion count measured before/after); gates stay green
